_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/12 8:45 AM  UPDATE:  We are working towards a limited opening with Chinook Express expected to open by 10am today. All other lifts and will be delayed with opening time TBA.
Sections of Highway 410 are still closed with large trees are down from last night's high winds. WSDOT crews are working to clear debris.  Refer to WSDOT for the most up-to-date highway status. 
  • Ski School will be closed for the day, and booked lessons will be rescheduled.
  • Rentals will be open in limited capacity.
  • The Mountain Commons Lodge will have a delayed opening. 
Expect further delays and potential operational impacts on the upper mountain today with high winds. 24 hour snow totals are yesterday's numbers and will be updated when time permits. Power is out on the mountain and webcams are currently unavailable. This storm system is sure showing up in force, and as always, safety is our top priority. 
 

Events: 
  • Military Appreciation Day is March 12th! Veterans, active military personnel, and their families receive 50% off of lift tickets and can be redeemed onsite in Guest Services. Start your morning with coffee and hot cocoa in the Gondola Plaza alongside our friends from Mission Outdoors. 
  • The Campbell Basin Rail Jam has moved to next Saturday March 14th!  From 11am-2pm throw down your cleanest lines, stomp your best tricks, and compete for some seriously sweet prizes from our stacked sponsors. Not competing? No stress — pull up, hype your crew, and catch all the action from Campbell Basin Lodge (elite viewing vibes included).
  •  This is the last weekend of night skiing coming up March 12th-15th! Take advantage of all the new snow on your schedule. Enjoy our Coca Cola Ride and Dine deal for just $75. Included is a night skiing ticket (1pm-7pm), dinner voucher and a Coke. 
Notes for today:
  1. Summit House will be closed March 11 and Rafters will be closed March 11 and 12 for private events. 
  2. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  3. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  4. Daily Reserves are now available for Saturdays and Sundays! 
  5. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  6. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  7. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  8. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 3/11/2026 6:00pm 

Forecaster: Morgan McGonagle
 
Synopsis: Hold on to your hats and your horses folks because the storm has arrived with fury. Freezing levels are hovering around 5500’ this evening, keeping things nice and juicy below mid-mountain, and will gradually slink down to 3500’ by the morning. Precipitation rates will be heaviest early in the evening as the winds are ramping up to extreme speeds with the potential to top 70mph. This storm continues to kick out of the Southwest, meaning Tahoma will take the brunt of the storm, but there should be plenty of snow to go around. Yeehaw!

Wednesday Night:
Sky Cover: Mixed precip transitioning to heavy snow
Freezing Elevation: 5500' decreasing to 3500'
12hr Precip: 7-10"
Wind Trends: Extreme WSW Gusting 70+ mph
Thursday:
Sky Cover: Cloudy and snowing
Freezing Elevation: 2500'
12hr Precip: 2-5"
Wind Trends: Strong WSW decreasing
Thursday Night:
Sky Cover: Cloudy and snowing
Freezing Elevation: 2500'
12hr Precip: 6-9” 
Wind Trends: Moderate to Strong WSW
Extended:   
On Friday the wind should decrease slightly while precipitation continues to flow in steadily. Tighten up your saddles and grip those reigns tight - we are smack in the bullseye of a very serious and volatile winter storm. 


*Daily Summaries: 12hr period representing 4am-4pm or 4pm-4am data
*Freezing Elevation (ft): Approximate elevation above sea level where the air temperature reaches 32 degree F

Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification
